meta.description: Coaching tips for Level 8: Automated Orchestration System

tips.tip: Use `claude -p` (headless mode) to batch-generate files — great for test generation, boilerplate, or migration scripts.
tips.example_before: Manually writing test files one by one in interactive mode
tips.example_after:
for model in User Product Order; do
  claude -p "Generate comprehensive tests for the $model model" > tests/test_${model}.py
done

tech_variants.react: claude -p 'Generate tests for all components in src/components/' > tests/components.test.tsx
tech_variants.python: claude -p 'Generate pytest tests for all models in src/models/' > tests/test_models.py
tech_variants.go: claude -p 'Generate table-driven tests for all handlers in internal/handlers/' > internal/handlers/handlers_test.go

tips.tip: Set up AI-powered PR review in your CI pipeline. Every PR gets an automated first-pass review before human review.
tips.example_before: Human reviewers catch everything, including trivial issues that waste review time
tips.example_after: GitHub Action on pull_request: claude reviews diff for bugs, security issues, style violations. Human reviewers focus on architecture.

tips.tip: Configure auto-fix pipelines: when CI fails, AI creates a fix PR automatically. Human still reviews before merge.
tips.example_before: CI fails → developer manually investigates → fixes → pushes → waits for CI again
tips.example_after: CI fails → AI analyzes failure → creates fix PR with `needs-review` label → developer reviews and merges. Saves 15-30 min per failure.

tips.tip: NEVER auto-merge AI-generated PRs. Always require human review. Use labels like `ai-generated` and `needs-review` for visibility.
tips.example_before: AI fix PR auto-merges to main branch
tips.example_after: AI fix PR created with labels: ai-generated, needs-review. Branch protection requires 1 human approval. Auto-merge disabled.

tips.tip: Use AI to auto-classify incoming issues: bug/feature/question, priority, affected component. Saves triage time at scale.
tips.example_before: Team manually reads every issue, assigns labels, estimates priority
tips.example_after: GitHub Action on issues.opened: AI reads issue, adds labels (bug/feature/question, priority, component), assigns to relevant team.

tips.tip: Set up cost monitoring for AI API usage. Track spend per workflow, set billing alerts, optimize expensive patterns.
tips.example_before: No idea how much AI API calls cost, surprised by the bill
tips.example_after: Dashboard tracking: $X/month on PR reviews, $Y/month on auto-fix, $Z/month on issue triage. Alert at 80% budget. Optimize the most expensive one.

tips.tip: Optimize AI costs: use cheaper models for simple tasks (triage, formatting), reserve expensive models for complex tasks (code review, auto-fix).
tips.example_after: Haiku for issue triage ($0.001/issue). Sonnet for PR review ($0.02/PR). Opus for complex auto-fix ($0.10/fix). Total: 60% cost reduction.

tips.tip: Track automation metrics weekly: auto-fix success rate, PR review accuracy, triage precision. If metrics drop, adjust prompts.
tips.example_before: Set up automation and forget about it
tips.example_after: Weekly review: auto-fix success 35% (target >30% ✅), PR review false positive rate 8% (target <10% ✅), triage accuracy 92%.
